deltas.releaseNoteText: Approval Engine v1 (REST API): Declarative workflow runtime for multi-step agent + human review and approvals. Author definitions, dispatch executions, and receive webhook events via new `/v2/workflow/definitions/*`, `/v2/workflow/executions/*`, and `/v2/workflow/steps/*` endpoints.
deltas.summary: Create a new rule covering all 14 Approval Engine REST endpoints under /v2/workflow/, including definitions CRUD, execution dispatch/cancel/list/get/events, and step recording/resolution/cancel/resolve — with key request/response fields, idempotency guidance, and webhook event delivery pattern.

skipped.item: ai/approval-engine/overview.mdx, setup.mdx, customize-behavior.mdx — frontend SDK components/hooks
skipped.reason: Approval Engine has no frontend SDK components, hooks, or props. All interaction is via REST API. No velt-setup-best-practices, velt-comments-best-practices, or other frontend skill is applicable.
Navigation structure only — no API or behavior change.
Minor prose changes only — no new API, webhook type, or behavior change documented in the diff.

skipped.item: REST API reference split (old overview.mdx deleted, per-method pages added)
skipped.reason: Documentation restructure only — same endpoint surface, same API contracts. The new per-method pages don't change the API; they just reorganize reference docs. All endpoint information is captured in delta-001.

gap_flags.description: No velt-approval-engine-best-practices skill exists. The Approval Engine is a significant new REST-only feature area (14 endpoints, parallel groups, quorum policies, SLA handling, event catalog). The delta above adds coverage to velt-rest-apis-best-practices as the closest existing home, but if the Approval Engine becomes a first-class product surface, a dedicated skill should be created.
gap_flags.recommendation: Consider creating a velt-approval-engine-best-practices skill once the API stabilizes (currently v1). For now, delta-001 adds a rest-approval-engine.md rule to velt-rest-apis-best-practices.
